Title: A SlMYB78-regulated bifunctional gene cluster for phenolamide and salicylic acid biosynthesis during tomato domestication, reducing disease resistance

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MicroTom; VF36; diverse accessions including BIG/CER/PIM
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: TS-21 (HapB); TS-108 (HapA); MicroTom/VF36 backgrounds used for validation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in tomato leaves; induced after Pst DC3000 infection, peaking around 8 h as part of BGC2
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; HXXXD catalytic motif
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Spermidine-derived phenolamide branch and isochorismate-derived SA branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of feruloyl-spermidine and higher feruloylated spermidines from feruloyl-CoA and spermidine; also promotes conversion of isochorismoyl-glutamate intermediate generated by ICS1/PBS3 into salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Feruloyl-CoA; spermidine; isochorismoyl-glutamate intermediate from chorismate via ICS1 and PBS3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of feruloyl-spermidine-derived phenolamides and salicylic acid, thereby enhancing resistance to Pst DC3000
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; recombinant enzyme assays; site-directed mutagenesis; overexpression; CRISPR knockout; haplotype comparison; metabolite profiling; pathogen inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified SlEPS1 in BGC2 as the key gene associated with diFer-Spd variation. 2. SlEPS1 is bifunctional, catalyzing both SA biosynthesis together with ICS1/PBS3 and phenolamide biosynthesis from Fer-CoA and spermidine. 3. Overexpression increased SA, Fer-Spd, diFer-Spd and resistance to Pst DC3000, while knockout reduced these metabolites and increased susceptibility. 4. The SlEPS1HapB allele has stronger catalytic activity and confers higher disease resistance than HapA, but was negatively selected during domestication.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Synergistically enhance antibacterial disease resistance against Pst DC3000; Fer-Spd also directly inhibits bacterial growth
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control component/core gene of BGC2; exact rate-limiting status not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 infection; exogenous SA; exogenous Fer-Spd
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disease-resistant HapB was negatively selected during domestication and is associated with wild accessions/smaller-fruit germplasm; no direct growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering or breeding to raise phenolamide and SA levels and improve bacterial disease resistance; preferential deployment of SlEPS1HapB allele is recommended
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: His169 in the BAHD HXXXD motif is critical for phenolamide catalytic activity and dual functionality; mutating His169 to Ser reduced phenolamide activity to AtEPS1-like levels but did not abolish SA-related activity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; GWAS using 2,318,614 SNPs in 401 tomato accessions; transcriptome co-expression analysis using published data; resequencing of 320 accessions

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly co-expressed with SlEPS1 in leaves; induced after Pst DC3000 infection, peaking around 8 h
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Feruloyl-CoA supply module within spermidine-derived phenolamide pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes methylation of caffeoyl-CoA using SAM to form feruloyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases precursor supply for feruloyl-spermidine-derived phenolamides and enhances resistance when transiently expressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein enzyme assay; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling; pathogen assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCoAOMT2 is a BGC2 component highly co-expressed with SlEPS1. 2. Recombinant SlCoAOMT2 converted Caf-CoA to Fer-CoA in vitro. 3. Transient expression increased Fer-Spd/diFer-Spd accumulation and enhanced resistance to Pst DC3000.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related phenolamides contributing to antibacterial resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential gene-stacking target with SlEPS1 and other BGC2 genes to elevate phenolamide-based resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the clustered BGC2 module

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly co-expressed with SlEPS1 in leaves; induced after Pst DC3000 infection, peaking around 8 h
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Feruloyl-CoA supply module within spermidine-derived phenolamide pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes methylation of caffeoyl-CoA using SAM to form feruloyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases precursor supply for feruloyl-spermidine-derived phenolamides and enhances resistance when transiently expressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein enzyme assay; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling; pathogen assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCoAOMT3 is a BGC2 component highly co-expressed with SlEPS1. 2. Recombinant SlCoAOMT3 converted Caf-CoA to Fer-CoA in vitro. 3. Transient expression increased Fer-Spd/diFer-Spd accumulation and enhanced resistance to Pst DC3000.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related phenolamides contributing to antibacterial resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential gene-stacking target with SlEPS1 and other BGC2 genes to elevate phenolamide-based resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the clustered BGC2 module

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly co-expressed with SlEPS1 in leaves; induced after Pst DC3000 infection, peaking around 8 h
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Feruloyl-CoA supply module within spermidine-derived phenolamide pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes methylation of caffeoyl-CoA using SAM to form feruloyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes feruloyl-spermidine-derived phenolamide accumulation and disease resistance; knockout reduces Fer-Spd/diFer-Spd and increases susceptibility to Pst DC3000
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein enzyme assay; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; CRISPR knockout; metabolite profiling; pathogen assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCoAOMT4 is a BGC2 component that synthesizes Fer-CoA from Caf-CoA. 2. Transient expression increased Fer-Spd and diFer-Spd and improved resistance. 3. CRISPR knockout reduced phenolamides and caused higher susceptibility to Pst DC3000.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related phenolamides contributing to antibacterial resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Validated engineering target for boosting phenolamide accumulation and pathogen resistance, especially in combination with SlEPS1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome co-expression; transcriptomics under multiple pathogen infections; metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only CoAOMT family member functionally validated by stable knockout in tomato

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Tomato WT and SlMYB78-RNAi lines; 35S:SlMYB78-GFP used for ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by infection with Pst DC3000 and other pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates BGC2 gene promoters and increases expression of BGC2 components
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances phenolamide and SA accumulation and improves resistance to Pst DC3000 by directly activating BGC2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive upstream regulator of the BGC2 cluster controlling coordinated production of both phenolamides and SA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; subcellular localization; promoter motif analysis; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; RNAi silencing; metabolite profiling; pathogen assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB78 was identified as the transcription factor most strongly co-expressed with SlEPS1. 2. It directly binds promoters of BGC2 genes and activates their transcription. 3. RNAi silencing reduced BGC2 expression, lowered SA/Fer-Spd/diFer-Spd accumulation, and increased susceptibility to Pst DC3000.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection including Pst DC3000 and multiple other pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory target to coordinately boost BGC2 expression, phenolamide/SA levels and disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome co-expression analysis using published data; pathogen-induced transcriptomic datasets; metabolomics
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text/figure legend contains one likely typo mentioning SlMYB13 in ChIP panel, but the study context and experiments support SlMYB78 as the focal regulator
